prs.title: feat: Rust recipe runner with transparent Python fallback
prs.author: rysweet

analysis.scope: Adds Rust recipe runner with transparent fallback to Python implementation
analysis.impact: high - modifies core recipe execution path

risk_factors.code_impact: high - changes recipe runner initialization and execution
risk_factors.test_impact: unknown - no visible test additions in changed files
risk_factors.backward_compatibility: good - transparent fallback to Python, opt-out available
risk_factors.security: medium - executes external binary, needs path validation
risk_factors.complexity: medium - new execution path with subprocess management

concerns: No tests visible in changed files (needs verification)
Security: subprocess execution of external binary
JSON parsing between Python and Rust boundary
Error handling at Python-Rust boundary needs review
CI status pending - no results yet
Large addition (1332 lines) suggests significant new code
testing_recommendations: Verify Python fallback works when Rust binary unavailable
Validate path search security (no code injection via PATH manipulation)
Verify behavior with malformed Rust binary output

triage_decision.rationale: Solid architectural approach with good backward compatibility, but introduces subprocess execution with security implications. Needs thorough testing of fallback mechanism, error handling, and security validations before merge. CI results pending.

prs.title: feat: integrate /top5 priority aggregation into pm-architect
prs.author: rysweet

analysis.scope: Integrates top5 priority aggregation into pm-architect skill
analysis.impact: medium - extends existing skill with new functionality

risk_factors.code_impact: medium - adds new script and patterns to existing skill
risk_factors.backward_compatibility: good - additive change, no breaking changes
risk_factors.complexity: medium - new aggregation logic with tests

positive_indicators: Includes 31 unit tests for new functionality
Additive change - no deletions to existing code
Integrates into existing skill rather than fragmenting
concerns: Integration with existing pm-architect patterns unclear
testing_recommendations: Test integration with existing pm-architect patterns

triage_decision.rationale: Well-structured feature with good test coverage. Architecture decision to integrate into pm-architect rather than standalone is sound. Needs review of test results and integration patterns before merge.

prs.title: fix: make .claude/ hooks canonical, replace amplifier-bundle/ copy with symlink
prs.author: rysweet

analysis.scope: Consolidates duplicate hooks by making .claude/ canonical and symlinking amplifier-bundle/
analysis.impact: high - massive file deletion (28K lines across 67 files)

risk_factors.test_impact: high - hooks are critical path for tool execution
risk_factors.backward_compatibility: high risk - symlink behavior differs across platforms
risk_factors.security: medium - symlink target must be validated
risk_factors.complexity: high - git symlink handling, platform differences

positive_indicators: Adds CI verification for symlink integrity
Already labeled triage:complete from previous review
concerns: Git symlink handling can be problematic
Deployment scripts may need symlink support
testing_recommendations: Test on all supported platforms (Windows, macOS, Linux)
Verify symlink resolution in amplifier-bundle context
Test git clone behavior with symlinks
Verify hooks still execute correctly via symlink
Test Windows symlink support (requires admin/developer mode)

triage_decision.rationale: Architecturally sound to eliminate duplication, but symlinks are notorious for platform-specific issues. The massive deletion is risky. Requires comprehensive platform testing before merge, especially on Windows. Already labeled high-risk appropriately.

prs.title: feat: distributed hive mind with DHT sharding + improved eval recall (51.2% → ≥83.9%)
prs.author: rysweet
prs.head: feat/distributed-hive-mind

analysis.scope: Major architectural change: distributed cognitive system with DHT sharding
analysis.impact: extreme - rewrites core cognitive adapter and agent system

risk_factors.test_impact: unknown - needs verification of test coverage
risk_factors.complexity: extreme - distributed systems, graph DB, eval improvements

positive_indicators: Already labeled and triaged as extreme-risk
4 days of updates showing active development
concerns: 146 files changed - nearly 15% of typical codebase
Mergeable state: DIRTY - merge conflicts exist
Distributed systems introduce new failure modes

triage_decision.rationale: This is a fundamental architectural change affecting core systems. The extreme scope and dirty merge state make this high-risk. Requires deep architectural review, comprehensive testing, and merge conflict resolution before considering merge. Already correctly labeled extreme-risk.

prs.title: feat: Fleet Orchestration — autonomous multi-VM coding agent management
prs.author: rysweet
prs.head: feat/fleet-orchestration

analysis.scope: Multi-VM fleet orchestration with Azure Bastion integration
analysis.impact: extreme - adds entirely new subsystem for fleet management

risk_factors.backward_compatibility: medium - additive feature, but infrastructure dependencies
risk_factors.security: high - SSH tunneling, Azure Bastion, multi-VM access
risk_factors.complexity: extreme - distributed system, concurrent SSH, TUI interface

positive_indicators: Addresses specific bug: concurrent SSH polling interference
Adds hostname verification for tunnel isolation
concerns: 8 days old - may need rebase soon
testing_recommendations: Failure mode testing (VM unreachable, tunnel failure)

triage_decision.rationale: Major new feature adding fleet orchestration capabilities. While mergeable state is clean and it fixes a documented issue, the extreme scope (28K+ lines) and infrastructure dependencies require deep review. Security audit needed for SSH/tunnel management. Already correctly labeled extreme-risk.
